    94→    94→def _field_values(item: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, dict[str, Any]]:
    95→    95→    values: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
    96→    96→    raw_values = ((item.get("fieldValues") or {}).get("nodes", []))
    97→    97→    if not isinstance(raw_values, list):
    98→    98→        return values
    99→    99→    for raw in raw_values:
   100→   100→        if not isinstance(raw, dict):
   101→   101→            continue
   102→   102→        field = raw.get("field") or {}
   103→   103→        name = field.get("name") if isinstance(field, dict) else None
   104→   104→        if not isinstance(name, str) or not name:
   105→   105→            continue
   106→   106→        value = next((raw[key] for key in ("name", "text", "number", "date", "title") if key in raw), None)
   107→   107→        values[name] = {
   108→   108→            "value": value,
   109→   109→            "updated_at": raw.get("updatedAt"),
   110→   110→            "field_id": field.get("id"),
   111→   111→            "option_id": raw.get("optionId") or raw.get("iterationId"),
   112→   112→        }
   113→   113→    return values
   114→   114→
   115→   115→
   116→   116→def _collect_remote(
   117→   117→    root: Path,
   118→   118→    nodes: list[dict[str, Any]],
   119→   119→    config: dict[str, Any],
   120→   120→    bd_bridge: Path,
   121→   121→    gh_bridge: Path,
   122→   122→) -> dict[str, Any]:
   123→   123→    remote: dict[str, Any] = {"schema_version": "1.0", "beads": {}, "github": {}}
   124→   124→    beads_to_graph = {
   125→   125→        str((node.get("beads_linkage") or {}).get("bd_issue_id")): _node_id(node)
   126→   126→        for node in nodes if (node.get("beads_linkage") or {}).get("bd_issue_id")
   127→   127→    }
   128→   128→    for node in nodes:
   129→   129→        node_id = _node_id(node)
   130→   130→        binding = node.get("tracker_binding")
   131→   131→        if binding == "beads":
   132→   132→            issue_id = (node.get("beads_linkage") or {}).get("bd_issue_id")
   133→   133→            if not issue_id:
   134→   134→                continue
   135→   135→            receipt = _invoke(
   136→   136→                [sys.executable, str(bd_bridge), "--repo-root", str(root), "--op", "show", "--bd-issue-id", str(issue_id)],
   137→   137→                root,
   138→   138→                f"Beads read {node_id}",
   139→   139→            )
   140→   140→            issue = receipt.get("result") or {}
   141→   141→            remote["beads"][node_id] = {
   142→   142→                "bd_issue_id": issue_id,
   143→   143→                "title": issue.get("title"),
   144→   144→                "status": issue.get("status"),
   145→   145→                "updated_at": issue.get("updated_at") or issue.get("updatedAt"),
   146→   146→                "depends_on": [beads_to_graph[value] for value in _dependency_ids(issue) if value in beads_to_graph],
   147→   147→            }
   148→   148→        elif binding == "github":
   149→   149→            linkage = node.get("issue_linkage") or {}
   150→   150→            repo, number = linkage.get("repo"), linkage.get("issue_number")
   151→   151→            if not repo or not number:
   152→   152→                continue
   153→   153→            receipt = _invoke(
   154→   154→                [sys.executable, str(gh_bridge), "--op", "issue-fetch", "--repo", str(repo), "--number", str(number)],
   155→   155→                root,
   156→   156→                f"GitHub Issue read {node_id}",
   157→   157→            )
   158→   158→            issue = receipt.get("result") or {}
   159→   159→            projects: dict[str, Any] = {}
   160→   160→            for project in node.get("github_project_linkages") or []:
   161→   161→                alias, project_id, item_id = project.get("project_alias"), project.get("project_id"), project.get("item_id")
   162→   162→                if not alias or not project_id or not issue.get("id"):
   163→   163→                    continue
   164→   164→                definitions: dict[str, Any] = {}
   165→   165→                owner = project.get("owner_login")
   166→   166→                number_value = project.get("project_number")
   167→   167→                if owner and number_value:
   168→   168→                    resolved = _invoke(
   169→   169→                        [
   170→   170→                            sys.executable, str(gh_bridge), "--op", "project-resolve",
   171→   171→                            "--owner", str(owner), "--project-number", str(number_value),
   172→   172→                        ],
   173→   173→                        root,
   174→   174→                        f"GitHub Project definition {node_id}/{alias}",
   175→   175→                    )
   176→   176→                    for field in (resolved.get("result") or {}).get("fields", {}).get("nodes", []):
   177→   177→                        if isinstance(field, dict) and field.get("name"):
   178→   178→                            definitions[str(field["name"])] = field
   179→   179→                found = _invoke(
   180→   180→                    [
   181→   181→                        sys.executable, str(gh_bridge), "--op", "project-item-find",
   182→   182→                        "--project-id", str(project_id), "--content-id", str(issue["id"]),
   183→   183→                    ],
   184→   184→                    root,
   185→   185→                    f"GitHub Project read {node_id}/{alias}",
   186→   186→                )
   187→   187→                items = (found.get("result") or {}).get("items", [])
   188→   188→                if len(items) > 1:
   189→   189→                    raise ContractError(f"duplicate Project items for {node_id}/{alias}")
   190→   190→                matching = items
   191→   191→                projects[str(alias)] = {
   192→   192→                    "item_id": matching[0].get("id") if matching else None,
   193→   193→                    "updated_at": matching[0].get("updatedAt") if matching else None,
   194→   194→                    "fields": _field_values(matching[0]) if matching else {},
   195→   195→                    "definitions": definitions,
   196→   196→                }
   197→   197→            remote["github"][node_id] = {**issue, "repo": repo, "projects": projects}
   198→   198→    return remote
   199→   199→
   200→   200→
   201→   201→def _remote_map(remote: dict[str, Any], binding: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
   202→   202→    raw = remote.get(binding, {})
   203→   203→    if isinstance(raw, list):
   204→   204→        return {
   205→   205→            str(item.get("graph_node_id") or item.get("external_ref")): item
   206→   206→            for item in raw if isinstance(item, dict) and (item.get("graph_node_id") or item.get("external_ref"))
   207→   207→        }
   208→   208→    if not isinstance(raw, dict):
   209→   209→        raise ContractError(f"remote state {binding} must be an object or array")
   210→   210→    return raw
   211→   211→
   212→   212→
   213→   213→def _status_from_remote(binding: str, remote: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
   214→   214→    if remote.get("deleted") is True:
   215→   215→        return "tombstoned"
   216→   216→    raw = str(remote.get("state") if binding == "github" else remote.get("status") or "").casefold()
   217→   217→    if raw in {"closed", "done"}:
   218→   218→        return "closed"
   219→   219→    if raw in {"blocked"}:
   220→   220→        return "blocked"
   221→   221→    return "active"
   222→   222→
   223→   223→
   224→   224→def _status_to_remote(binding: str, status: str) -> str:
   225→   225→    if binding == "github":
   226→   226→        return "closed" if status in {"done", "closed", "tombstoned"} else "open"
   227→   227→    return {"done": "closed", "closed": "closed", "tombstoned": "closed", "blocked": "blocked"}.get(status, "open")
   228→   228→
   229→   229→
   230→   230→def _choose(
   231→   231→    *,
   232→   232→    node_id: str,
   233→   233→    field: str,
   234→   234→    local: Any,
   235→   235→    remote: Any,
   236→   236→    base: dict[str, Any] | None,
   237→   237→    local_updated: Any,
   238→   238→    remote_updated: Any,
   239→   239→    confirmation: str | None,
   240→   240→) -> str:
   241→   241→    if local == remote:
   242→   242→        return "equal"
   243→   243→    if base is None:
   244→   244→        local_changed = remote_changed = True
   245→   245→    else:
   246→   246→        local_changed = local != base.get("local")
   247→   247→        remote_changed = remote != base.get("remote")
   248→   248→        if not local_changed and not remote_changed:
   249→   249→            return "equal"
   250→   250→        if local_changed and not remote_changed:
   251→   251→            return "local"
   252→   252→        if remote_changed and not local_changed:
   253→   253→            return "remote"
   254→   254→        if not local_changed and not remote_changed:
   255→   255→            return "equal"
   256→   256→    if confirmation in {"local", "remote"}:
   257→   257→        return confirmation
   258→   258→    local_time, remote_time = _time(local_updated), _time(remote_updated)
   259→   259→    if local_time and remote_time and local_time != remote_time:
   260→   260→        return "local" if local_time > remote_time else "remote"
   261→   261→    if base is None and local_time and not remote_time:
   262→   262→        return "local"
   263→   263→    if base is None and remote_time and not local_time:
   264→   264→        return "remote"
   265→   265→    return "conflict"
   266→   266→
   267→   267→
   268→   268→def _config_projects(config: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, dict[str, Any]]:
   269→   269→    projects = ((config.get("github") or {}).get("projects", [])) if isinstance(config, dict) else []
   270→   270→    return {
   271→   271→        str(project.get("alias")): project
   272→   272→        for project in projects if isinstance(project, dict) and project.get("alias")
   273→   273→    }
   274→   274→
   275→   275→
   276→   276→def _confirmations(values: list[str]) -> dict[tuple[str, str], str]:
   277→   277→    result: dict[tuple[str, str], str] = {}
   278→   278→    for value in values:
   279→   279→        try:
   280→   280→            identity, choice = value.rsplit("=", 1)
   281→   281→            node_id, field = identity.split(":", 1)
   282→   282→        except ValueError as exc:
   283→   283→            raise ContractError("--confirm must be NODE:FIELD=local|remote") from exc
   284→   284→        if choice not in {"local", "remote"} or not node_id or not field:
   285→   285→            raise ContractError("--confirm must be NODE:FIELD=local|remote")
   286→   286→        result[(node_id, field)] = choice
   287→   287→    return result
   288→   288→
   289→   289→
   290→   290→def _plan(
   291→   291→    nodes: list[dict[str, Any]],
   292→   292→    remote: dict[str, Any],
   293→   293→    snapshot: dict[str, Any],
   294→   294→    config: dict[str, Any],
   295→   295→    confirmations: dict[tuple[str, str], str],
   296→   296→) -> dict[str, Any]:
   297→   297→    imports: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
   298→   298→    exports: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
   299→   299→    conflicts: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
   300→   300→    tombstones: list[dict[str, Any]] = []

80→
    81→
    82→def repository_eval_root(root: Path) -> Path:
    83→    """Return a real repository-owned eval-log directory, rejecting symlink roots."""
    84→    authority = root.resolve(strict=True)
    85→    logical = authority / "eval-log"
    86→    if logical.is_symlink():
    87→        raise ContractError("repository eval-log root must not be a symbolic link")
    88→    logical.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    89→    resolved = contained(logical, authority, must_exist=True)
    90→    if not resolved.is_dir():
    91→        raise ContractError("repository eval-log root is not a directory")
    92→    return resolved
    93→

820→                            "reason": str(projection_error),
   821→                        })
   822→        for pending_item in plan["pending_retry"]:
   823→            if not pending_item.get("alias"):
   824→                continue
   825→            current_graph = load_json(graph_path)
   826→            current_node = next(
   827→                (item for item in current_graph.get("nodes", []) if _node_id(item) == pending_item["node"]),
   828→                None,
   829→            )
   830→            if current_node is None:
   831→                continue
   832→            pending_patch = _project_linkage_patch(
   833→                current_node,
   834→                {"alias": pending_item["alias"], "kind": "pending-retry"},
   835→                None,
   836→                error=str(pending_item.get("reason") or "pending_retry"),
   837→            )
   838→            if pending_patch:
   839→                projection_receipt = _apply_patch(root, pending_item["node"], pending_patch, upsert)
   840→                applied.append({
   841→                    "type": "local_pending_retry", "node": pending_item["node"],
   842→                    "alias": pending_item["alias"], "receipt": projection_receipt,
   843→                })
   844→        if remote_path is not None and any(item["type"] == "external" for item in applied):
   845→            atomic_json(remote_path, remote)
   846→        if not plan["conflicts"] and not plan["pending_retry"] and not failed:
   847→            atomic_json(snapshot_path, plan["next_snapshot"])
   848→
   849→    graph_after = graph_path.read_bytes()
   850→    remote_after = remote_path.read_bytes() if remote_path is not None else None
   851→    snapshot_after = snapshot_path.read_bytes() if snapshot_path.exists() else None
   852→    if args.dry_run and graph_after != graph_before:
   853→        raise ContractError("dry-run changed the canonical graph")
   854→    if args.dry_run and remote_after != remote_before:
   855→        raise ContractError("dry-run changed the remote fixture")
   856→    if args.dry_run and snapshot_after != snapshot_before:
   857→        raise ContractError("dry-run changed the sync snapshot")
   858→    report = {
   859→        "ok": not failed,
   860→        "mode": "apply" if args.apply else "dry-run",
   861→        "imports": plan["imports"],
   862→        "exports": plan["exports"],
   863→        "conflicts": plan["conflicts"],
   864→        "tombstones": plan["tombstones"],
   865→        "pending_retry": [*plan["pending_retry"], *failed],
   866→        "changes": len(plan["imports"]) + len(plan["exports"]) + len(plan["tombstones"]),
   867→        "write_count": len(applied),
   868→        "applied": applied,
   869→        "graph_sha256_before": _sha(graph_before),
   870→        "graph_sha256_after": _sha(graph_after),
   871→        "remote_sha256_before": _sha(remote_before),
   872→        "remote_sha256_after": _sha(remote_after),
   873→        "snapshot_sha256_before": _sha(snapshot_before),
   874→        "snapshot_sha256_after": _sha(snapshot_after),
   875→        "converged": not plan["imports"] and not plan["exports"] and not plan["conflicts"] and not plan["pending_retry"] and not failed,
   876→        "executed_at": utc_now(),
   877→    }
   878→    if not args.no_eval_log and args.eval_log:
   879→        eval_root = repository_eval_root(root)
   880→        target = Path(args.eval_log)
   881→        target = target if target.is_absolute() else root / target
   882→        target = contained(target, eval_root, must_exist=False)
   883→        atomic_json(target, report)
   884→        report["eval_log"] = target.relative_to(root).as_posix()
   885→    dump(report)
   886→    return 0 if not failed else 1
   887→
   888→
   889→if __name__ == "__main__":
   890→    try:
   891→        raise SystemExit(main())
   892→    except (ContractError, OSError, json.JSONDecodeError, KeyError, TypeError, ValueError) as exc:
   893→        dump({"ok": False, "error": str(exc), "write_count": 0})
   894→        raise SystemExit(2)
   895→

content.content:
720→def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
   721→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
   722→    parser.add_argument("--repo-root", default=".")
   723→    parser.add_argument("--graph")
   724→    parser.add_argument("--remote-state")
   725→    parser.add_argument("--snapshot", default=".dev-graph/state/sync-snapshot.json")
   726→    mode = parser.add_mutually_exclusive_group(required=True)
   727→    mode.add_argument("--dry-run", action="store_true")
   728→    mode.add_argument("--apply", action="store_true")
   729→    parser.add_argument("--confirm", action="append", default=[])
   730→    parser.add_argument("--eval-log")
   731→    parser.add_argument("--no-eval-log", action="store_true")
   732→    parser.add_argument("--bd-bridge")
   733→    parser.add_argument("--gh-bridge")
   734→    parser.add_argument("--upsert-node")
   735→    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
   736→
   737→    root = Path(args.repo_root).expanduser().resolve(strict=True)
   738→    config = load_json(root / ".dev-graph" / "config.json")
   739→    if not isinstance(config, dict):
   740→        raise ContractError(".dev-graph/config.json must be an object")
   741→    graph_path = _graph_path(root, args.graph, config)
   742→    ensure_no_pending_transaction(graph_path)
   743→    graph_before = graph_path.read_bytes()
   744→    graph = json.loads(graph_before.decode("utf-8"))
   745→    nodes = graph.get("nodes") if isinstance(graph, dict) else None
   746→    if not isinstance(nodes, list) or not all(isinstance(node, dict) for node in nodes):
   747→        raise ContractError("canonical graph must contain nodes[] objects")
   748→    snapshot_path = _state_path(root, args.snapshot, ".dev-graph/state/sync-snapshot.json", must_exist=False)
   749→    snapshot_before = snapshot_path.read_bytes() if snapshot_path.exists() else None
   750→    snapshot = json.loads(snapshot_before.decode("utf-8")) if snapshot_before is not None else {"schema_version": "1.0", "nodes": {}}
   751→    bd_bridge = Path(args.bd_bridge).resolve() if args.bd_bridge else Path(__file__).with_name("bd-bridge.py")
   752→    gh_bridge = Path(args.gh_bridge).resolve() if args.gh_bridge else Path(__file__).with_name("gh-bridge.py")
   753→    upsert = Path(args.upsert_node).resolve() if args.upsert_node else Path(__file__).with_name("upsert-node.py")
   754→
   755→    remote_path: Path | None = None
   756→    if args.remote_state:
   757→        remote_path = _state_path(root, args.remote_state, "", must_exist=True)
   758→        remote_before = remote_path.read_bytes()
   759→        remote = json.loads(remote_before.decode("utf-8"))
   760→    else:
   761→        remote_before = None
   762→        remote = _collect_remote(root, nodes, config, bd_bridge, gh_bridge)
   763→    if not isinstance(remote, dict):
   764→        raise ContractError("remote state must be an object")
   765→    plan = _plan(nodes, remote, snapshot, config, _confirmations(args.confirm))
   766→    applied: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
   767→    failed: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
   768→    if args.apply:
   769→        for node_id, patch in sorted(plan["patches"].items()):
   770→            try:
   771→                applied.append({"type": "local", "node": node_id, "receipt": _apply_patch(root, node_id, patch, upsert)})
   772→            except ContractError as exc:
   773→                failed.append({"type": "local", "node": node_id, "reason": str(exc)})
   774→        by_id = {_node_id(node): node for node in nodes}
   775→        fixture_now = utc_now()
   776→        for operation in plan["exports"]:
   777→            try:
   778→                if remote_path is not None:
   779→                    fixture_receipt = _fixture_apply(remote, operation, fixture_now)

50→
    51→def dump(value: Any) -> None:
    52→    print(json.dumps(value, ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True, indent=2))
    53→
    54→
    55→def atomic_json(path: Path, value: Any) -> None:
    56→    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    57→    fd, temp = tempfile.mkstemp(prefix=f".{path.name}.", dir=str(path.parent))
    58→    try:
    59→        with os.fdopen(fd, "w", encoding="utf-8") as stream:
    60→            json.dump(value, stream, ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True, indent=2)
    61→            stream.write("\n")
    62→            stream.flush()
    63→            os.fsync(stream.fileno())
    64→        os.replace(temp, path)
    65→    finally:
    66→        try:
    67→            os.unlink(temp)
    68→        except FileNotFoundError:
    69→            pass
    70→
    71→
    72→def contained(path: Path, root: Path, *, must_exist: bool = True) -> Path:
    73→    candidate = path.resolve(strict=must_exist)
    74→    authority = root.resolve(strict=True)
    75→    try:
    76→        candidate.relative_to(authority)
    77→    except ValueError as exc:
    78→        raise ContractError(f"path escapes authority root: {candidate} not within {authority}") from exc
    79→    return candidate
    80→
    81→
    82→def repository_eval_root(root: Path) -> Path:
    83→    """Return a real repository-owned eval-log directory, rejecting symlink roots."""
    84→    authority = root.resolve(strict=True)
    85→    logical = authority / "eval-log"
    86→    if logical.is_symlink():
    87→        raise ContractError("repository eval-log root must not be a symbolic link")
    88→    logical.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    89→    resolved = contained(logical, authority, must_exist=True)

   569→def _apply_patch(root: Path, node_id: str, patch: dict[str, Any], upsert: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
   570→    request_dir = repository_eval_root(root) / "dev-graph-sync-requests"
   571→    request_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

   576→            [sys.executable, str(upsert), "--repo-root", str(root), "--input", str(request)],
   577→            root,
   578→            f"C02 node patch {node_id}",
   579→        )
   580→    finally:

content.content:
66→def _invoke(argv: list[str], root: Path, label: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
    67→    cp = run(argv, cwd=root, check=False)
    68→    if cp.returncode:
    69→        raise ContractError(f"{label} failed ({cp.returncode}): {(cp.stderr or cp.stdout).strip()}")
    70→    try:
    71→        result = json.loads(cp.stdout)
    72→    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
    73→        raise ContractError(f"{label} returned invalid JSON") from exc
    74→    if not isinstance(result, dict):
    75→        raise ContractError(f"{label} receipt must be an object")
    76→    return result
    77→
    78→
    79→def _dependency_ids(issue: dict[str, Any]) -> list[str]:
    80→    result: list[str] = []
    81→    for item in issue.get("dependencies", []):
    82→        if isinstance(item, dict):
    83→            dependency = item.get("id")
    84→            relation = item.get("dependency_type") or item.get("type")
    85→            if relation not in (None, "blocks"):
    86→                continue
    87→        else:
    88→            dependency = item
    89→        if isinstance(dependency, str) and dependency:
    90→            result.append(dependency)
    91→    return sorted(set(result))
    92→
    93→
    94→def _field_values(item: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, dict[str, Any]]:
    95→    values: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
